---
title: Customizing theme
description: Modify colors, typography, and other design tokens to match your brand.
---

Your theme is the foundation of your design system—design tokens that define colors, typography, borders, corners, and shadows across your entire project. Design tokens are reusable values that create consistency. Update a token once, and every component using it updates automatically.

Your theme exports as Tailwind CSS configuration. When you sync components, Subframe generates `tailwind.config.js` or `theme.css` with all your tokens. Components use standard Tailwind classes like `text-brand-primary`—values come from your Subframe theme.

## Accessing your theme

Navigate to theme settings:

1. Click **Theme** tab in the top navigation
2. Theme editor shows five sections: Colors, Typography, Borders, Corners, Shadows

All changes save automatically and apply immediately to components.

<img src="https://developers.elementor.com/docs/assets/img/elementor-placeholder-image.png" alt="The Subframe interface showing the Components tab selected in the top navigation, with Theme highlighted in the left sidebar, and the theme editor panel showing Colors, Typography, Borders, Corners, and Shadows sections" />

## Quick styles

Apply professionally designed themes instantly with **Edit theme** button:

1. Click **Edit theme** in Colors section
2. Browse 10 preset styles (Linear, Google, Stripe, Spotify, VS Code, etc.)
3. Select a style to apply brand colors, fonts, and corner radius
4. Toggle between Light and Dark variants
5. Customize individual sections after applying

Each preset configures:
- Brand and neutral color palettes
- Semantic colors (error, success, warning)
- Typography (heading and body fonts with weights)
- Corner radius preset (none, small, medium, large)

## Ask AI to generate theme

Use Ask AI to generate custom themes when existing presets don't match your vision. Press <kbd>Cmd</kbd> + <kbd>K</kbd> and describe your desired theme. AI modifies colors, typography, shadows, and corner radius based on your description.

**What AI modifies:**
- Brand and neutral color palettes
- Typography (heading fonts with weights from Google Fonts)
- Shadow stacking and elevation
- Border radius values

**How it works:**
1. Press <kbd>Cmd</kbd> + <kbd>K</kbd> to open Ask AI
2. Describe your desired theme: "Create a modern dark theme with purple accents" or "Apply Stripe's design style"
3. AI generates modified theme configuration
4. Review and manually apply the proposed changes

AI theme generation affects your entire project. Use sparingly for major theme updates. For minor adjustments, manually edit individual tokens.

## Colors

Subframe organizes colors into two types: **palettes** and **individual color tokens**.

### Palettes vs individual tokens

**Color palettes** are collections of related color shades based on Tailwind presets:
- Contain 10 shades numbered 50-900 (50 lightest, 900 darkest)
- Shades maintain mathematical relationships for consistency
- Cannot reference other tokens (no aliases)
- Default palettes (Brand, Neutral, Error, Success, Warning) can be renamed but not deleted
- Custom palettes can be created, imported to, and deleted

**Individual color tokens** are standalone semantic colors:
- Used for specific UI purposes (Brand Primary, Default Font, Subtext Color, etc.)
- Can reference other tokens as aliases (e.g., Brand Primary → Brand 700)
- Can be created with custom names
- Default individual tokens cannot be deleted

Use palettes for color scales that need consistent relationships. Use individual tokens for semantic colors that might reference palette shades.

### Editing palettes

Click the palette icon to swap entire palette:

1. Click palette icon next to palette name
2. Select from Tailwind color presets
3. Palette updates all shades instantly

Brand palette offers 18 color options. Neutral palette offers 5 gray variations (Slate, Gray, Zinc, Neutral, Stone).

### Editing individual tokens

Click any color token to open color picker. Changes save automatically and apply to all components using that token.

### Creating custom palettes and tokens

**New palette:**
1. Click **New palette** button
2. Name your palette
3. Add color tokens to palette
4. Import CSS variables (see [Importing tokens](/theme/importing-tokens))

**New individual token:**
1. Click **+** button in individual tokens section
2. Name your token (use descriptive names like `accent-purple`)
3. Set color value via color picker

<Warning>
Deleting a custom token converts all references to hex values. This breaks theme connection—components won't update when you change colors.
</Warning>

## Typography

Configure fonts and weights in **Edit theme** dialog or Typography section:

**Heading font** — Select from 10+ Google Fonts (Inter, Geist, Outfit, etc.). Adjust weight slider (300-800).

**Body font** — Currently shares same font as heading. Independent body font coming soon.

**Font shuffle** — Click **Shuffle** to randomly select fonts by category: Rounded, Geometric, Humanist, Monospace.

Typography tokens (Heading 1-3, Body, Caption, Code) auto-update when you change font settings.

## Borders, corners, shadows

**Borders** — Default border width (1px, 2px, 3px). Adjust in Borders section.

**Corners** — Four presets in **Edit theme**:
- None (0px all sizes)
- Small (2px/4px/8px for sm/md/lg)
- Medium (4px/8px/12px)
- Large (8px/16px/24px)

**Shadows** — Five elevation presets: sm, md, lg, overlay, default. Each preset editable with multiple shadow layers.

## Syncing theme changes to code

After updating your theme, sync the changes to your codebase:

```bash
npx @subframe/cli sync --all
```

This updates your `tailwind.config.js` or `theme.css` file with the new token values. All components in your codebase will automatically use the updated theme.

## Limitations

**Spacing not configurable** - Spacing tokens (padding, margins, gaps) use Tailwind defaults. Custom spacing values not supported.

**Shared heading/body font** - Both use same font currently. Independent body font selection coming soon.

## Best practices

**Use semantic names** - Name custom tokens based on purpose, not appearance. Use `accent-color` instead of `purple-500`.

**Test across components** - After significant changes, review components for proper contrast and appearance.

**Use palettes over individual tokens** - Palettes maintain relationships between shades automatically.
